meta_title: Roofing in Kings Point, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Kings Point, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile, 170mph wind-rated. Free estimates, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing Services for Kings Point, Florida Homeowners
intro:
Kings Point is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved active adult communities, where well-kept condominiums and manicured grounds reflect the pride residents take in their homes. But beneath the beauty of this established neighborhood lies a real and ongoing concern: aging rooftops. With approximately 70% of Kings Point homes built before 1994, a significant portion of the community is covered by roofing systems that predate Florida's modern hurricane-era building standards. That means thousands of residences in the 33426 ZIP code are operating with roofs that were never designed to meet today's 170mph wind-load requirements — and every storm season is a gamble with that reality.

The Kings Point Condominiums represent one of the most densely developed residential footprints in western Boynton Beach, and while the community benefits from attentive HOA management and shared amenities near the Kings Point Clubhouse and Kings Point Theater, individual unit owners and association boards alike must contend with the practical realities of South Florida's climate. Positioned approximately six miles from the Atlantic coastline, Kings Point sits in a wind corridor that channels tropical energy inland during hurricane events. While the area is not classified as High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Palm Beach County's wind zone designation still mandates hurricane-rated roofing products for all new installations and replacements — a code requirement that directly affects every homeowner considering a roof upgrade.

With an average home value of approximately $220,000, Kings Point residents understand the importance of protecting their investment wisely. A failing or outdated roof doesn't just risk interior water damage — it can trigger insurance non-renewals, suppress resale value, and expose residents to costly emergency repairs after a storm. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Palm Beach County homeowners navigate the roofing process from inspection to final permit sign-off. Whether you're managing a unit in the Kings Point Condominiums or handling roofing needs on behalf of an association board, our team brings the local knowledge, licensed expertise, and premium product lines needed to protect what matters most.

section_why.h2: Why Kings Point Residents Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on a simple but powerful promise: exceptional service at every stage of the project. Known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' we bring more than 60 years of combined installation experience to every job we take on — and in a community like Kings Point, where the homes carry real history and residents have high expectations, that experience is not just a selling point. It's a necessity.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from competitors is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our roofing crews. Every technician who climbs onto a Kings Point rooftop is a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee — vetted, trained, and accountable to the same quality standards we've upheld across 500+ completed homes in the region. This matters enormously in the roofing industry, where subcontracted labor is common and quality control often suffers as a result. When we commit to a project in the 33426 area, our own team sees it through from tear-off to final inspection.

We are fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, carry an A+ BBB rating, and have established a working knowledge of the Palm Beach County Building Division's permitting requirements that allows us to manage the compliance process seamlessly on your behalf. For Kings Point homeowners — many of whom are retirees looking for a low-hassle, high-quality experience — that turnkey service is invaluable. We pull the permits, coordinate the inspections, and keep you informed throughout.

Our product offerings are specifically selected for South Florida conditions. We install CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems — both rated to meet or exceed Palm Beach County's 170mph wind zone standards. Whether your Kings Point home features a low-slope design or a pitched tile roof, we have a solution engineered for durability and local code compliance.

section_history.h2: Kings Point's Storm History and What It Means for Your Roof Today
section_history.body:
Kings Point has weathered more than its share of serious storms since 1990, with at least seven named tropical systems impacting the area and three leaving particularly lasting impressions on the community. Hurricane Frances swept through Palm Beach County in September 2004, bringing sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that exposed vulnerabilities in roofing systems throughout the region. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma struck in October 2005 — arguably the most destructive storm Palm Beach County had seen in decades — with sustained winds that tore shingles, cracked tile, and overwhelmed roofing systems that had already been weakened by Frances. The one-two punch of those consecutive seasons accelerated the deterioration of countless rooftops in the Kings Point area.

Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, a storm that reinforced just how vulnerable pre-1994 roofing systems remain. Irma's approach up the Florida peninsula brought damaging wind gusts throughout Boynton Beach and the surrounding communities, causing widespread roof damage that resulted in a surge of insurance claims and emergency repair calls across Palm Beach County. For Kings Point homeowners still living under roofs installed in the 1980s or early 1990s, Irma was a stark reminder that deferred maintenance and aging materials are a dangerous combination in this part of Florida.

The cumulative stress of these storms — even for roofs that were not visibly destroyed — accelerates granule loss, fastener corrosion, and underlayment degradation in ways that may not be immediately visible but significantly reduce a roof's ability to perform under the next major event. If your Kings Point home has not had a professional roof inspection or replacement since before 2005, the probability of hidden structural compromise is high. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers complimentary assessments to help you understand exactly where your roof stands.

section_code.h2: Palm Beach County Building Codes and Roofing Compliance in Kings Point
section_code.body:
Roofing work in Kings Point falls under the jurisdiction of the Palm Beach County Building Division, and Florida's post-Andrew building code evolution means that any roofing project — whether a full replacement or a significant repair — must comply with the 2023 Florida Building Code as adopted and enforced by the county. While Kings Point is not classified as a High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), this should not be misunderstood as a lower standard. Palm Beach County's wind speed design requirement of 170mph means that all roofing products and installation methods must still be rated and installed in accordance with stringent hurricane-resistance protocols.

Under current Florida Building Code, roofing materials installed in Palm Beach County must meet specific uplift resistance requirements, use approved underlayment systems, and be fastened according to manufacturer specifications validated for the local wind zone. For tile roofing systems, this means mortar-set or mechanically fastened tiles with proper hip and ridge reinforcement. For shingle systems, this means minimum six-nail fastening patterns on architectural shingles in high-wind applications. Non-compliant installation methods — even with quality materials — can void manufacturer warranties and result in failed inspections.

Permits for roofing work in Kings Point must be obtained through the Palm Beach County Building Division prior to the start of any project. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the entire permitting process on behalf of our clients, including documentation of product approvals, submission of roofing specifications, and coordination of required inspections. For Kings Point residents in the 33426 ZIP code, particularly those in condominium associations that may have additional approval layers, our experience navigating both county and HOA requirements ensures your project moves forward without delays or compliance issues.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake Roofing Systems Built for Kings Point Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier product lines specifically chosen for their performance in South Florida's demanding climate: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark Series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines carry the certifications and wind ratings necessary for compliance with Palm Beach County's 170mph wind zone designation, and both are available with manufacturer warranties that provide long-term peace of mind for Kings Point homeowners.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series is one of the most respected architectural shingle lines in North America, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles offer a Class A fire rating, ENERGY STAR compliance for solar reflectance, and enhanced wind resistance up to 110mph standard with upgraded installation methods achieving higher ratings applicable to Palm Beach County requirements. The Landmark Series provides a natural wood-shake appearance with the durability and low maintenance profile that active adult communities in Kings Point demand. With a range of colors that complement the architectural character of Kings Point Condominiums, CertainTeed shingles offer both function and curb appeal.

For Kings Point homes with tile roofing — whether original concrete tile from the 1980s or clay tile that needs replacement — West Lake's concrete and clay tile systems represent the gold standard in South Florida durability. West Lake tiles are manufactured to withstand the thermal cycling, UV exposure, and wind-driven rain that define the Palm Beach County environment year after year. Their tile systems are compatible with high-wind installation methods, and their profiles are available in styles ranging from flat to barrel — ideal for maintaining the aesthetic integrity of established communities like Kings Point while bringing the roof into full compliance with modern code standards.

section_cost.h2: What Kings Point Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in a New Roof
section_cost.body:
Understanding the cost of a new roof in Kings Point begins with recognizing the variables at play: the size and pitch of your roof, the material system selected, the extent of any underlying deck damage, and whether HOA or permit coordination adds complexity to the project. For a typical Kings Point condominium or single-family home with an average value around $220,000, a full roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark Series architectural shingles generally ranges from $9,000 to $16,000 depending on square footage and condition. West Lake tile systems, given the additional material and labor investment, typically range from $15,000 to $28,000 for a complete replacement.

These figures represent real, lasting value for Kings Point homeowners. A new hurricane-rated roof is one of the highest-ROI home improvements available in South Florida, consistently delivering returns through reduced insurance premiums, improved energy efficiency, and enhanced resale value. For a home valued at $220,000, even a mid-range roofing investment can yield measurable returns within the first three to five years when insurance savings are factored in.

For homeowners concerned about the upfront cost, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. Our financing program is designed to make it possible for Kings Point residents on fixed incomes or retirement budgets to access the same quality roofing systems we install for any other Palm Beach County homeowner — without delay and without compromising on materials or craftsmanship. We believe every homeowner deserves a roof that performs when it matters most, regardless of what the budget looks like today.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Kings Point Roofing Projects
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for investing in a new hurricane-rated roof in Kings Point is the impact it has on homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been under significant stress in recent years, with carriers tightening eligibility requirements and raising rates — particularly for homes with older roofs. In Palm Beach County, many major carriers now restrict coverage or require expensive riders for homes with roofs that are 15 years or older. A new roof can mean the difference between maintaining affordable coverage and facing non-renewal.

Beyond eligibility, a new roof that meets Florida Building Code requirements for Palm Beach County's wind zone opens the door to meaningful premium reductions through wind mitigation credits. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs a qualifying roofing system, homeowners are eligible for a wind mitigation inspection — a formal evaluation that documents the roof's construction features to the insurer. Credits are available for roof deck attachment method, roof-to-wall connections, roof covering type, and roof shape. Collectively, these credits can reduce the wind portion of a homeowner's premium by 20% to 45% in many cases, generating annual savings of several hundred dollars.

Carriers actively writing policies in the Kings Point area and throughout Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits when calculating premiums. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's roofing installations are documented with the necessary specifications to support a wind mitigation inspection immediately upon project completion. We encourage every Kings Point homeowner we work with to share that report with their insurance agent promptly, as the savings can begin with the very next policy renewal.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Kings Point home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: The most reliable way to determine this is through a professional inspection. Homes in Kings Point built before 1994 — which represent roughly 70% of the community — often have roofing systems past their useful life expectancy, especially after storms like Irma and Wilma. Signs such as widespread granule loss, cracked or missing tiles, sagging decking, or active leaks typically indicate a full replacement is more cost-effective than ongoing repairs. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers complimentary roof assessments to help you make an informed decision.

faqs.q: What does a new roof cost in Kings Point and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Kings Point home, CertainTeed architectural shingle systems generally range from $9,000 to $16,000, while West Lake tile systems range from $15,000 to $28,000 depending on roof size and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. Our team can walk you through financing terms during your free estimate so you can choose the approach that best fits your budget.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Kings Point, and how does the process work?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all roof replacements in Kings Point through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from submitting product approvals and roofing specifications to coordinating required county inspections. Homeowners in Kings Point Condominiums may also need HOA approval, which we can assist in documenting. Our team ensures full compliance with the 2023 Florida Building Code and Palm Beach County's 170mph wind zone requirements.

faqs.q: How long does a roofing project typically take in Kings Point?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in Kings Point are completed within one to three days once materials are on-site and permits are approved. The permitting timeline through Palm Beach County Building Division typically adds one to two weeks before work begins. Complex projects involving tile systems, extensive deck repair, or condominium coordination may take slightly longer. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides a clear project timeline during the estimate process and keeps homeowners updated throughout every phase of the job.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance premium in Kings Point?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof qualifies your Kings Point home for a wind mitigation inspection, which documents features that earn credits with your insurance carrier. These credits can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 20% to 45% annually. Additionally, many carriers in Palm Beach County impose surcharges or restrict coverage for older roofs, so a replacement often eliminates those penalties. We recommend sharing your completed wind mitigation report with your insurance agent immediately after your new roof is installed.
